economic_hypothesis: A static union of ten to twelve positive-cost-resilient sleeves deliberately spanning distinct market/session cells should increase accepted non-overlapping opportunities and shared-account target velocity over event-count-matched market-concentrated unions under identical account risk limits, because independent session liquidity demands are paid by different participant populations.
who_pays: Participants demanding immediacy in distinct futures market/session cells pay each sleeve edge; the account union monetizes their non-synchronous arrival rather than increasing per-signal leverage.
persistence_reason: Session-specific hedging and inventory transfer recur structurally across products while the fixed shared-account conflict and MLL limits prevent unconstrained beta accumulation.
structural_change_from_0013: replace contemporaneous same-direction scaling with static market/session coverage membership
increase union breadth to 10-12 sleeves while retaining one risk unit per accepted event
matched control changes membership concentration rather than confirmation graphs
no cross-market confirmation threshold and no density scaling
mandatory_null: Same-size membership with total source event count matched within 15 percent but concentrated in fewer market/session cells, identical account limits, starts, costs and execution semantics.
CONTRE: Broader market/session coverage can merely add more correlated beta and transaction costs. Event-count matching cannot perfectly match latent component quality, so paired uplift must remain economically large, stable and MLL-safe.
